reasoning: The statistical model provides a primary anchor of 46%. While recent momentum for the 1-year yield has been strongly upward (rising consecutively for 6 weeks up to 3.79% on March 27), the 2-year treasury yield completely flattened out between March 20 (3.88%) and March 27 (3.88%). This flattening of the 2-year yield suggests that the short-to-medium duration rates have priced in the recent news of hotter-than-expected inflation and Fed decisions, and the upward pressure has stalled. Consequently, there is a lower likelihood that the 1-year yield will continue to increase significantly over the next week. Following the historical base rate (around 50%), the statistical model's anchor (46%), and the recent stalling in the 2-year yield, I estimate a 46% probability that the 1-year yield will increase by April 5.
